title: Avoid array index out-of-bounds access
description: Accessing an array element with an invalid index will throw an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException and can cause program crashes or unpredictable behavior. Always ensure that array indices are within valid bounds before accessing elements. This helps prevent runtime errors and improves code reliability.
recommendation: Check that array indices are within valid bounds before accessing elements.

noncompliant:
int[] arr = new int[5];
int x = arr[5]; // Noncompliant: index out of bounds
